general: Dogs are social animals that thrive on routine. Daily care includes feeding, exercise, grooming, and mental stimulation. Fresh water should always be available.
puppy_kitten: Puppies (under 1 year) need 3-4 small meals per day, frequent potty breaks every 2-3 hours, socialization with people and other animals, and basic obedience training sessions of 5-10 minutes. Vaccinations: DHPP series at 8, 12, and 16 weeks; rabies at 16 weeks.
adult: Adult dogs (1-7 years) need 2 meals per day, 30-60 minutes of exercise daily, annual wellness exams, and dental cleanings. Heartworm prevention monthly. Flea and tick prevention monthly.
senior: Senior dogs (8+ years) need 2 smaller meals per day of senior-formula food, shorter but more frequent walks, bi-annual vet exams, and joint supplements if recommended by vet. Monitor for hearing/vision changes.
feeding: Feed based on weight: under 10 lbs = 1/4-1 cup/day; 10-25 lbs = 1-2 cups/day; 25-50 lbs = 2-3 cups/day; 50-75 lbs = 3-4 cups/day; over 75 lbs = 4-5 cups/day. Split into 2 meals. Always use weight-appropriate food formula.
exercise: Exercise needs by size: small dogs (under 20 lbs) = 20-30 min/day; medium dogs (20-50 lbs) = 30-60 min/day; large dogs (50+ lbs) = 60-90 min/day. Include walks, playtime, and mental enrichment toys.
grooming: Brush coat 2-3 times per week (daily for long-haired breeds). Bathe every 4-6 weeks. Trim nails every 3-4 weeks. Clean ears weekly. Brush teeth daily or at minimum 3x per week.
vet_checkups: Annual wellness exam for adults. Bi-annual for seniors (8+ years). Core vaccines: rabies (every 1-3 years), DHPP (every 1-3 years). Optional: bordetella (every 6-12 months for social dogs), leptospirosis, Lyme. Monthly heartworm and flea/tick prevention.
